mnemonics: Clusters Are Dense Neighbourhoods (DBSCAN): Helps remember that DBSCAN forms clusters from dense neighborhoods of points separated by low-density regions.
keyPoints: DBSCAN can find clusters of arbitrary shape and handle noise.
It requires two parameters: epsilon (radius) and minPts (minimum points).
Points are classified as core, border, or noise points based on their local density.
commonMistakes: Confusing border points with noise points; noise points are not part of any cluster, while border points are.
Improper parameter selection (epsilon and minPts) leading to all points labeled as noise or one giant cluster.
